# `energy/state_electricity_peak_demand_mw`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: Annual peak electricity demand (by state)  
**One-line**: The single highest 15-minute system demand the state's grid served at any moment during the fiscal year.  
**Source artifact last modified**: 2026-05-16 (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_electricity_peak_demand_mw.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/state_electricity_peak_demand_mw.json)

## Definition

The single highest 15-minute system demand the state's grid served at any moment during the fiscal year. The companion API field 'Peak Demand Date' tells you when it occurred — almost always a hot afternoon for southern/western states and a cold morning for northern/north-eastern states.

## Signature

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Entity kind | `state` |
| Time grain | `fiscal_year` |
| Value kind | `raw` |
| Unit | `MW` |
| Direction | `neutral` |
| Comparability | `comparable_with_normalisation` |
| Attribution geography | `where_administered` |
| Implementing authority | `state` |
| Scale hint | `linear` |

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2017-04..2025-04 (9 periods)
- **Spatial**: All-India aggregate; 28 states; 5 UTs (34 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 305

## Methodology vintage

NITI Aayog ICED state-wise deep-dive API; payload fetched 2026-05-14T07:51:49Z; 9 fiscal years, 305 rows.

## Notes

Source: NITI Aayog ICED dashboard, row 'Peak Demand'. Underlying figures published by CEA. The accompanying 'Peak Demand Date' string is not ingested as a separate indicator (it would need value_kind=raw and date semantics).
